author: @Gwydion67
text:
Great Rob, thanks a lot! 😊
I love such easy recipes for clays if all kind. I love making both good-looking and useful things out of (mineral) clay, and also learned about paper clay in early school days. 🧻⚱😄
Different purposes, different recipes. 👍

author: @dolfinmagikpro
text:
Great video and information!  Thank you for sharing!
Just curious ... What is the approximate working time?  How long until fully cured?  Would heat speed up the curing?
Thanks again ... Have a better day!

replies.author: @SodiumInteresting
replies.text: how long does pva take to set

replies.author: @The-Green-Manali-shi
replies.text: @SodiumInteresting Do not apply excessive heat. Depends on how thick it is applied. 5mm thickness should dry in 2 to 3 days at room temp.

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text: @The-Green-Manali-shi G, did you try adding 2%-20% of robs conductive ink blends in there, copper gel to, to get a simple copper sheen & strength to your creations! 'the art is the -diy Nano- battery' lol

author: @thornhedge9639
text:
Although I occasionally disagree with you Robert; I admire your character.   When a subject doesn't interest me I skip watching; when it does I always find illuminating content.    You really are a treasure Robert.
  By the way; your friends recipe is a variant of "Paper Mache Clay", and what he gave you took way more than a cup of loo role! :)

author: @dav1dbone
text: Could try it as a replacement for the terracotta pot in the zinc battery, or as an ion separator in an electroplating bath, not being limited  to a specific shape if you made a few thin tiles, they could be stacked up.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't think so mate - or at least you would have to mix something else in there as it dries as opposed to setting

author: @darrellpidgeon6440
text: Always looking for new recipes for air-dry clay.  This one is similar to one I want to try,where one of the ingredients is wall-joint compound.  For some reason,  the DAP brand is not suitable.  Toilet paper is so good to use because of the percentage of cotton it contains.  I remember as a kid, the street behind us was being worked on.  They dug a huge hole, piling the dirt on the side.  There was a lot of natural clay, which I collected.  I had so much fun making all kinds of things.  Even full-sized heads.  You should try to make a chess set with that lot of clay.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: a chess set is an awesome idea mate - thanks for the idea - cheers

author: @CitizenSmith50

author: @arne6787
text: Use casein glue instead and mix a little Portland cement in to make it more structural and durable, shredded paper fibers might also  help.  Water and fireproof, insulation for your RV?  Lightweight and more foamlike would also be advantageous.  Workable, moldable at first, rock hard and smoothable once cured.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
